question.text: Under Section 11(4)(e), what is the maximum period for attachment of bank accounts?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11(4)(e) allows SEBI to attach bank accounts or other property of any intermediary or person involved in violations of the SEBI Act for a period not exceeding 90 days. **The Trap:** The 90-day limit is specific to this interim measure. Other protective measures under 11(4) may not have the same time limit. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Bank account attachment is a powerful tool — it preserves assets during investigation. Compliance officers should note that even association with violations can trigger attachment.
